INSUFFICIENT SHOWING IN THIS DEMURRER. DEFENDANT MUST SPECIFY HOW AND WHY RES JUDICATA IS APPLICABLE, REQUEST JUDICIAL NOTICE OF SPECIFIC PLEADINGS, AND ATTACH COPIES OF ALL PLEADINGS TO BE COMPARED AND CONSIDERED BY THE COURT.) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EDUARDO PANIAGUA, and, PANIAGUA CONSTRUCTION INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: John P. Hurabiell, Esq. of Huppert & Hurabiell (415) 387-3001, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Lucy Bedolla Mejia, Esq. of the Mejia Law Firm, (650) 616-8400, appearing for defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with sixteen days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts that show complaint is not barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP section 335.1). (Issue of res judicata not addressed without prejudice to being raised. Insufficient showing in this demurrer. Defendant must specify how and why res judicata is applicable, request judicial notice of specific pleadings, and attach copies of all pleadings to be compared and considered by the Court.) Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
